title: Add richer API-side filters for session inspection
summary: Started shaping richer server-side filters so the frontend can query session status, branch families, and text search without downloading everything first.
goal: Prepare the dashboard and future command palette for larger handoff corpora by moving more filtering work into the route layer.
changesMade: Mapped filter inputs needed by sessions_context and the dashboard shell
Outlined branch, status, and text filter behavior
Reviewed where data.ts can own filtering without multiplying route logic
Identified places where the frontend still assumes a full corpus load

features.name: route-side session filtering
features.why: Larger frontend corpora will eventually need narrower payloads for speed and clarity.

features.name: command palette filter feed
features.why: The palette should be able to search the same filtered dataset the shell is showing.

features.name: branch family awareness
features.why: Grouping frontend sessions by branch family helps when several related efforts are active at once.

decisions.decision: Keep filtering primitives in lib/data.ts even if the route exposes them
decisions.why: One shared implementation reduces drift between API and direct file-based reads.

decisions.decision: Support status and query first, branch groupings second
decisions.why: Those two filters unlock the biggest inspection gains with the smallest surface area.

blockers: The shell still expects the whole session list, so route filtering needs a small frontend contract pass too.
openQuestions: Should filter state live in the URL before we add server-side filtering?
resumePrompt: Continue API filtering in dashboard/src/lib/data.ts, then update dashboard/src/app/api/data/route.ts once the filter contract is settled.
